description: A designed square graphic from ICE's recurring 'THE WORST FIRST' arrest-card series. The full background is a high-contrast desaturated/black-and-white close-up photograph of a pair of human hands held out, palms up, locked in metal handcuffs, evoking a captured suspect. A dark gradient overlays the left side so white and pale-cyan text reads clearly. Top-left headline in light cyan small-caps serif reads 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL', with a white subline '2/12/2025 | Detroit, MI'. A red diagonal banner across the top-right corner reads 'DETROIT' in white serif caps. Mid-left, in large bold white sans-serif, the arrestee's name: 'Juan Antonio Tamayo-Beltran'. Below that a bold white label 'CRIME:' followed by a bulleted list: 'Heroin trafficking conspiracy'. Bottom-left shows the round blue-and-white DHS seal next to the white serif 'ICE' wordmark. Bottom-right is a solid red box with white serif text reading '-THE- WORST FIRST'. The propaganda technique pairs an unnamed generic handcuffed-hands stock image (no actual photo of the person) with the named individual and his alleged crime, framing immigration enforcement as the removal of dangerous criminals; the city banner localizes it for regional/local-news amplification. Target audience: general public and local media, to build support for mass deportation by emphasizing 'criminal alien' threats.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 2/12/2025 | Detroit, MI; Juan Antonio Tamayo-Beltran; CRIME: Heroin trafficking conspiracy; DETROIT; -THE- WORST FIRST; ICE
